name: Chirag Chawla
degree: B.Tech + M.Tech — Department of Industrial Chemistry
tagline: I break models for breakfast, tune them over lunch, and debug for dessert.
bio: AI researcher at IIT (BHU) Varanasi obsessed with making AI safer, smarter, and more honest. I work across post-training alignment, reasoning, interpretability, learning on graphs, and active learning — with active papers on LLM fatigue, multi-agent systems, and biomedical NLP. I don't just study AI, I ship it — 7+ industry roles, and consult early-stage founders on building with AI. Hardcore researcher at heart: if there's an unsolved problem, I'm already thinking about it.
funFacts: 🚗 Certified car enthusiast — can talk torque curves and gradient descent in the same breath
🧮 Solves math puzzles for fun (yes, really)
☕ Runs on coffee and CUDA kernels
🎯 IIT student who accidentally became an AI researcher
🔬 Industrial Chemistry → AI Research: the most unexpected career arc

highlights: Co-first author of AlignTune (arXiv 2026), modular post-training alignment toolkit for LLMs
Integrated SFT and RLHF-style methods (GRPO, PPO, IFT, DPO) with composable reward functions
Building safety guardrails and critic-based decoding for inference-time alignment
Extended after 6 months for strong performance
Co-authored PERCS: Persona-Guided Controllable Biomedical Summarization Dataset (arXiv:2512.03340)
Researching Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL) for language generation
Modified Hugging Face GenerationMixin for agent-aware sampling and dynamic decoding policies
Explored lookahead and agent-aware decoding strategies for multi-agent coherence
Promoted from AI Research Intern to CTO, leading agentic AI division
Designed AI solutions and implementation roadmaps for B2B clients
Led teams developing practical, deployable AI systems
Advised startups on AI strategy, product decisions, and state-of-the-art techniques
Collaborated with IIT BHU seniors on MVP development
Researched configuration hardening security for enterprise environments
Investigated black-box and white-box attack strategies on AI systems
Designed benchmarks for AI model security against adversarial attacks
Research under Prof. Anil Kumar Singh on gender in Hindi grammatical systems
Applied canonical correlation analysis for gender classification in inanimate nouns
Developed machine translation systems for low-resource languages
Built agentic RAG frameworks with autonomous retrieval and generation agents
Developed multimodal architectures integrating text, images, and documents
Integrated Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for improved multimodal understanding
Developed AI models to minimize production scrap in industrial environments
Implemented LSTM and ARIMA models for forecasting and anomaly detection
Built AWS + Airflow pipelines with Docker/Kubernetes for MLOps

projects.title: AlignTune
projects.description: Modular post-training alignment toolkit for reasoning-focused LLMs integrating SFT, GRPO, PPO, IFT, and DPO with composable reward functions.

projects.title: PERCS Dataset
projects.description: Persona-guided controllable biomedical summarization dataset with persona-tailored summaries for clinicians, policymakers, patients, and students.

projects.title: Multimodal RAG with CLIP & OpenAI
projects.description: Image-and-text RAG pipeline using CLIP ViT-L/14, FAISS vector store, and GPT-4o. FastAPI + Streamlit demo with streamed responses.

projects.title: MRI → CT Image Translation (VAE–CycleGAN)
projects.description: Model to convert MRI images to CT images using a VAE–CycleGAN architecture, with epistemic uncertainty shaping in latent space for safer medical deployment.

projects.title: Fourier Neural Operator (2D DarcyFlow PDE)
projects.description: Implemented FNO for solving 2D DarcyFlow PDE. Progressive training from coarse to fine resolution with spectral feature visualization.

projects.title: Implicit Deep Equilibrium CNN
projects.description: Convolutional model using Deep Equilibrium Models (DEQ) via TorchDEQ with implicit solver for infinite-depth transformations in constant memory.
